concepts_list.name: domain_Omega
concepts_list.description: The main computational domain in which the physical problem is defined.
concepts_list.justification: The domain of interest is defined as a region Ω ⊂ R^d, and it is repeatedly referenced in integrals over the domain and its boundaries. This establishes it as the primary spatial domain of the problem.

concepts_list.name: test_function_v
concepts_list.description: A test function used in the weak formulation, belonging to the function space V, and is varied over the domain to satisfy the weak form.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol v appears in multiple integrals and is used in the weak form as a test function for all v ∈ V. It is central to the variational formulation and is used in both the left-hand and right-hand sides of the equations.

concepts_list.name: thermal_conductivity_kappa
concepts_list.description: The thermal conductivity tensor, which governs the heat conduction in the domain and appears in the gradient term of the weak form.
concepts_list.justification: The thermal conductivity κ is explicitly mentioned as a given parameter in the thermal boundary value problem and appears in the term ∇v ⋅ (∇κ u) in the weak form.

concepts_list.name: primal_variable_u
concepts_list.description: The primary unknown field (e.g., temperature or displacement) that is sought in the weak formulation, with the same symbol used across different physical problems.
concepts_list.justification: The variable u is used to denote the temperature in the thermal problem and the displacement in the elastostatics problem. The paper explicitly states that 'the symbol u will be used to denote the primal variable irrespective of the type of problem'.

concepts_list.name: domain_integral_d_Omega
concepts_list.description: The differential element representing the integration over the domain Ω.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol dΩ appears in integrals over the domain Ω and is explicitly used in the weak form as the differential element for volume integration.

concepts_list.name: heat_source_f
concepts_list.description: The internal heat source term in the thermal boundary value problem.
concepts_list.justification: The heat source f is given as input and appears in the right-hand side of the weak form equation as ∫_Ω v f dΩ.

concepts_list.name: neumann_boundary_Gamma_t
concepts_list.description: The part of the boundary where Neumann boundary conditions (prescribed heat flux) are applied.
concepts_list.justification: The boundary Γ_t is explicitly defined as the region where Dirichlet and Neumann BCs are prescribed, respectively, and appears in the integral over the Neumann boundary.

concepts_list.name: heat_flux_q_bar
concepts_list.description: The prescribed heat flux on the Neumann boundary Γ_t.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol q (with bar notation) is referred to as the prescribed heat flux and appears in the boundary integral over Γ_t as ∫_{Γ_t} v q dΓ.

concepts_list.name: boundary_integral_d_Gamma
concepts_list.description: The differential element representing integration over a boundary surface.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol dΓ appears in the boundary integrals over Γ_t and other boundaries, indicating it is the differential element for surface integration.

concepts_list.name: test_space_V
concepts_list.description: The function space of test functions, which is used in the weak formulation and must contain functions that satisfy the necessary boundary conditions.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol V is defined as the space of test functions, and the weak form is stated to hold for all v ∈ V. It is used in the formulation and discretization process.
